title: System and method for outputting an indicator representative of the effects of stimulation provided to a subject during sleep
abstract: The present disclosure pertains to a system configured to output an indicator representative of effects of stimulation provided to a subject during a sleep session. The indicator is determined based on a combination of the effect of stimulation on sleep restoration, stimulation quality, sleep architecture factors, and/or other information. The indicator is determined using age matched reference information on deep sleep duration and EEG slow wave activity. The contribution to the indicator associated with sleep architecture factors is determined based on age matched reference information including sleep onset latency, wake after sleep onset, total sleep time, micro- arousal count, sleep stage(s) prior to awakening, and/or other information.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A system (10) configured to output an indicator representative of effects of stimulation provided to a subject during a sleep session, the system comprising: one or more stimulators (16) configured to provide the stimulation to the subject during the sleep session; one or more sensors (18) configured to generate output signals conveying information related to brain activity in the subject during the sleep session; and one or more hardware processors (20) operatively communicating with the one or more stimulators and the one or more sensors, the one or more hardware processors configured by machine-readable instructions to: determine, based on the output signals and the stimulation provided to the subject: a slow wave activity metric indicative of a cumulative amount of slow wave activity in the subject during the sleep session; a stimulation quality metric indicative how well the stimulation enhances slow wave activity in the subject during the sleep session; and a sleep architecture metric indicative of a sleep quality for the subject during the sleep session; combine the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric to determine the indicator; and output the indicator for display to the subject. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more hardware processors are further configured to: obtain age matched reference information for the subject that indicates information related to reference amounts of cumulative slow wave activity during sleep sessions, reference levels of stimulation provided during sleep sessions, and reference 

 levels of sleep quality during sleep sessions for a population of subjects similar in age to the subject; and determine the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric based on the reference information. 3. The system of claim 2, where the one or more sensors comprise electroencephalogram (EEG) electrodes, and wherein the one or more hardware processors are configured such that: the slow wave activity metric is determined based on a cumulative slow wave activity factor and a non-rapid eye movement (NREM) duration factor; the cumulative slow wave activity and NREM factors are both determined based on the output signals and the age matched reference information; and the cumulative slow wave activity factor is determined based on cumulative EEG power in a 0.5 to 4 Hz band or a sub-band within the 0.5 to 4 Hz band across detected NREM epochs during the sleep session. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the one or more hardware processors are configured such that the stimulation quality metric is determined based on one or both of a number of tones delivered to the subject during the sleep session and a number of tones at a specific volume delivered to the subject during the sleep session, and the age matched reference information. 5. The system of claim 2, wherein the one or more hardware processors are configured such that the sleep architecture metric is determined based on the age matched reference information and one or more of a sleep onset latency value for the subject, a wake after sleep onset value for the subject, a total sleep time during the sleep session, or a number of arousals during the sleep session, and 

 wherein the sleep onset latency value, the wake after sleep onset value, the total sleep time, and/or the number of arousals are determined based on the output signals. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more hardware processors are configured such that the combination comprises a linear combination of the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric; and the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric are individually weighted in the linear combination. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the one or more hardware processors are configured to cause display of the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric without combining them to determine the indicator. 8. A method for outputting an indicator representative of effects of stimulation provided to a subject during a sleep session with an indicator system (10), the system comprising one or more stimulators (16), one or more sensors (18), and one or more hardware processors (20), the method comprising: providing, with the one or more stimulators, the stimulation to the subject during the sleep session; generating, with the one or more sensors, output signals conveying information related to brain activity in the subject during the sleep session; determining, with the one or more processors, based on the output signals and the stimulation provided to the subject: a slow wave activity metric indicative of a cumulative amount of slow wave activity in the subject during the sleep session; a stimulation quality metric indicative how well the stimulation enhances slow wave activity in the subject during the sleep session; and 

 a sleep architecture metric indicative of a sleep quality for the subject during the sleep session; combining, with the one or more processors, the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric to determine the indicator; and outputting, with the one or more hardware processors, the indicator for display to the subject. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising: obtaining, with the one or more hardware processors, age matched reference information for the subject that indicates information related to reference amounts of cumulative slow wave activity during sleep sessions, reference levels of stimulation provided during sleep sessions, and reference levels of sleep quality during sleep sessions for a population of subjects similar in age to the subject; and determining, with the one or more hardware processors, the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric based on the reference information. 10. The method of claim 9, where the one or more sensors comprise electroencephalogram (EEG) electrodes, and wherein: the slow wave activity metric is determined based on a cumulative slow wave activity factor and a non-rapid eye movement (NREM) duration factor; the cumulative slow wave activity and NREM factors are both determined based on the output signals and the age matched reference information; and the cumulative slow wave activity factor is determined based on cumulative EEG power in a 0.5 to 4 Hz band or a sub-band within the 0.5 to 4 Hz band across detected NREM epochs during the sleep session. 1 1. The method of claim 9, wherein the stimulation quality metric is determined based on one or both of a number of tones delivered to the subject during the sleep session and a number of tones at a specific volume delivered to the subject during the sleep session, and the age matched reference information. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein the sleep architecture metric is determined based on the age matched reference information and one or more of a sleep onset latency value for the subject, a wake after sleep onset value for the subject, a total sleep time during the sleep session, or a number of arousals during the sleep session, and wherein the sleep onset latency value, the wake after sleep onset value, the total sleep time, and/or the number of arousals are determined based on the output signals. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein the combination comprises a linear combination of the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric; and the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric are individually weighted in the linear combination. 14. The method of claim 8, further comprising causing display of the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric without combining them to determine the indicator. 15. A system for outputting an indicator representative of effects of stimulation provided to a subject during a sleep session, the system comprising: means (16) for providing the stimulation to the subject during the sleep session; means (18) for generating output signals conveying information related to brain activity in the subject during the sleep session; 

 means (20) for determining, based on the output signals and the stimulation provided to the subject: a slow wave activity metric indicative of a cumulative amount of slow wave activity in the subject during the sleep session; a stimulation quality metric indicative how well the stimulation enhances slow wave activity in the subject during the sleep session; and a sleep architecture metric indicative of a sleep quality for the subject during the sleep session; means (20) for combining the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric to determine the indicator; and means (20) for outputting the indicator for display to the subject. 16. The system of claim 15, further comprising: means (20) for obtaining age matched reference information for the subject that indicates information related to reference amounts of cumulative slow wave activity during sleep sessions, reference levels of stimulation provided during sleep sessions, and reference levels of sleep quality during sleep sessions for a population of subjects similar in age to the subject; and means (20) for determining the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric based on the reference information. 17. The system of claim 16, where the means for generating output signals comprise electroencephalogram (EEG) electrodes, and wherein: the slow wave activity metric is determined based on a cumulative slow wave activity factor and a non-rapid eye movement (NREM) duration factor; the cumulative slow wave activity and NREM factors are both determined based on the output signals and the age matched reference information; and 

 the cumulative slow wave activity factor is determined based on cumulative EEG power in a 0.5 to 4 Hz band or a sub-band within the 0.5 to 4 Hz band across detected NREM epochs during the sleep session. 18. The system of claim 16, wherein the stimulation quality metric is determined based on one or both of a number of tones delivered to the subject during the sleep session and a number of tones at a specific volume delivered to the subject during the sleep session, and the age matched reference information. 19. The system of claim 16, wherein the sleep architecture metric is determined based on the age matched reference information and one or more of a sleep onset latency value for the subject, a wake after sleep onset value for the subject, a total sleep time during the sleep session, or a number of arousals during the sleep session, and wherein the sleep onset latency value, the wake after sleep onset value, the total sleep time, and/or the number of arousals are determined based on the output signals. 20. The system of claim 15, wherein the combination comprises a linear combination of the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric; and the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric are individually weighted in the linear combination. 21. The system of claim 15, further comprising means (20) for causing display of the slow wave activity metric, the stimulation quality metric, and the sleep architecture metric without combining them to determine the indicator.
